generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal temperature sensor measuring real-time temperature inside the insulated styrofoam box for the incubator system. Provides high-resolution thermal data with ±0.1°C precision."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor measuring real-time temperature inside the insulated styrofoam box for redundancy and improved spatial coverage of thermal gradients."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient conditions outside the insulated styrofoam box to capture environmental influences on the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Time-series temperature measurements from the first internal sensor transmitted via low-latency protocol (e.g., MQTT) with high temporal resolution (1-10s intervals)."
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Time-series temperature measurements from the second internal sensor transmitted synchronously with InternalTemperatureData1 for cross-validation."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Time-series ambient temperature measurements transmitted asynchronously when threshold deviations occur or periodically (1-10s intervals)."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalDynamicsModelTwoParameter : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model representing thermal dynamics of the incubator with two free parameters for heat transfer and thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, SimulationEngine
]

instance ThermalDynamicsModelFourParameter : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced physics-based differential equation model with four free parameters demonstrating superior accuracy in modeling thermal inertia and transient behaviors."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, SimulationEngine
]

instance SensorTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated raw sensor data from all temperature sensors (internal/external) preprocessed for noise filtering and normalization before model integration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance HistoricalTemperatureLogs : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing historical temperature measurements and model calibration records for trend analysis and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Least-squares optimization and Bayesian inference algorithms for calibrating thermal models (two/four parameters) using real-time sensor data to align DT predictions with PT behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation environment (MATLAB/Simulink or SciPy) executing calibrated thermal models to predict system behavior under varying conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService, WhatIfAnalysisService
]

instance ControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controllers (PID, MPC) and state estimators (Kalman filters) for real-time temperature regulation and fault detection in the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureControlService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB or TimescaleDB instance storing sensor telemetry and historical logs with high write/read throughput for real-time analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance EdgeComputingPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based edge computing node for low-latency processing of sensor data and local execution of control algorithms before cloud synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, LocalControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection and processing of sensor data from the Physical Twin to track operational state and detect deviations in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight, SensorAnomalyInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of calibrated thermal models to reproduce and predict the Physical Twin's behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight, ControlParameterRecommendation
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of monitoring algorithms to identify safety violations (e.g., overheating, sensor failures) and trigger corrective actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight, CorrectiveActionRecommendation
]

instance TemperatureControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization of temperature regulation using supervisory controllers to minimize energy consumption while maintaining target conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight, TemperatureAdjustmentAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Proactive identification of wear-and-tear patterns or component degradation through data-driven algorithms and model updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentDegradationInsight, MaintenanceScheduleRecommendation
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Periodic recalibration of thermal models using real-time sensor data to maintain alignment between Digital Twin and Physical Twin behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, CalibrationParameterUpdate
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation of hypothetical scenarios (e.g., environmental changes) to evaluate system robustness and optimize operational parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioOutcomeInsight, ParameterAdjustmentRecommendation
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of interactive visualizations (e.g., temperature gradients, historical trends) for engineering communication and operational transparency."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureGradientVisualization, HistoricalTrendVisualization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time internal/external temperature readings with spatial and temporal context for operational monitoring."
]

instance SensorAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of sensor malfunctions or data inconsistencies between internal/external temperature measurements."
]

instance ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted thermal behavior of the incubator under current conditions or simulated scenarios using calibrated models."
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of safety-critical deviations (e.g., overheating beyond thresholds) requiring immediate attention."
]

instance ComponentDegradationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive detection of component wear (e.g., sensor drift, heatbed inefficiency) based on performance trends and model discrepancies."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of model accuracy through comparison of DT predictions with real-time PT measurements."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of energy consumption patterns and recommendations for efficiency improvements in temperature regulation."
]

instance TemperatureAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of heatbed/fan parameters to maintain target temperature within ±0.5°C tolerance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CorrectiveActionRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested corrective measures (e.g., emergency cooling, operator alert) for detected safety violations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CalibrationParameterUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated update of model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia coefficients) based on calibration algorithm results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommended maintenance intervals or component replacements based on degradation insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TemperatureGradientVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of temperature distribution inside the incubator box with real-time updates."
]

instance HistoricalTrendVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series plots of temperature measurements and model predictions for comparative analysis and trend identification."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heating element responsible for generating and distributing heat inside the insulated container under control algorithm directives."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Air circulation fan ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the incubator box to prevent thermal gradients."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with sensors and actuators (heatbed, fan) to execute control algorithms and manage system operations."
]

// C1: Environment and Components
instance IncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:SystemComponent [
    base:desc "Physical Twin system comprising an insulated styrofoam box, heating element, air circulation fan, and embedded sensors for temperature regulation."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinOrchestrationLayer : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Unified orchestration layer coordinating specialized sub-models (thermal dynamics, control logic) and services across the Digital Twin architecture."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance ThermalSubModel : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sub-model handling thermal dynamics calculations and calibration within the broader Digital Twin framework."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance ControlSubModel : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sub-model implementing supervisory controllers and state estimators for real-time temperature regulation."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Computational environment hosting the Digital Twin, including edge (Raspberry Pi) and cloud components for distributed execution."
    base:contains DigitalTwinOrchestrationLayer, ThermalSubModel, ControlSubModel, TimeSeriesDatabase, EdgeComputingPlatform
]

instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the incubator system with sensors, actuators, and embedded controller."
    base:contains IncubatorPhysicalTwin, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, RaspberryPiController
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of system state and performance metrics to detect operational deviations and ensure safety compliance."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStateInsight, SensorAnomalyInsight
]

instance LocalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Local execution of control algorithms on edge devices to provide low-latency responses to system changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureAdjustmentAction
]

instance ControlParameterRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended adjustments to control parameters based on simulation results and system performance metrics."
]

instance ScenarioOutcomeInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system behavior under hypothetical scenarios to evaluate robustness and optimize operational parameters."
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to system parameters based on what-if analysis results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT system involves **control commands** generated by the DT’s **algorithms** (e.g., supervisory controllers, optimizers) and transmitted to the **Physical Twin (PT)** to regulate its behavior. The **command types** include **setpoint adjustments** for the **heatbed** (e.g., PWM signals or duty-cycle modulation) and potentially **fan speed control** (if dynamically adjustable). The **control message format** is not explicitly detailed, but it likely follows a **structured protocol** (e.g., **Modbus TCP, CAN bus, or proprietary serial commands**) to ensure compatibility with the **Raspberry Pi controller**. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not described, but **safety checks** (e.g., bounds on temperature setpoints) are implied to prevent physical damage. **Feedback systems** operate via **closed-loop control**: the DT monitors the PT’s temperature sensors and adjusts commands iteratively to minimize error. **Conflict resolution** may involve **priority-based arbitration** (e.g., safety overrides for overheating) or **consensus algorithms** if multiple controllers are involved. **Command execution confirmation** is likely achieved via **acknowledgment signals** from the PT (e.g., Raspberry Pi confirming receipt and execution of commands)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system operates with a **real-time synchronization paradigm**, where data from the Physical Twin (PT) is continuously collected via sensors (e.g., temperature sensors in the incubator case study) and fed into the DT framework. The temporal granularity depends on the **sampling rate of the sensors** and the **latency requirements of the calibration algorithms** used to update the models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. For instance, in the incubator example, temperature sensors provide **continuous monitoring**, implying a high-frequency data acquisition loop (e.g., sub-second or second-level granularity) to ensure accurate model calibration and real-time monitoring of system performance. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** that dynamically adjust model parameters based on the latest sensor data, ensuring the digital representation remains synchronized with the PT's state. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **control loop of the CPS**, where delays in data processing or model updates could degrade system responsiveness (e.g., in temperature regulation). The DT’s time scale is thus **event-driven and continuous**, with critical dependencies on the **speed of data acquisition, model update frequency, and algorithmic processing time** to maintain predictive and operational fidelity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system follows a **service-oriented architecture (SOA) pattern**, where key components—**data acquisition, models, and algorithms**—are integrated into a cohesive framework to enable real-time interaction with the Physical Twin (PT). The orchestration is centered around a **closed-loop control structure** (as depicted in Figure 1), where:
  1. **Data** from the PT (e.g., sensor readings) is ingested and preprocessed.
  2. **Models** (e.g., differential equations for dynamics, thermal models) are calibrated using algorithms to align with the PT’s current state.
  3. **Monitors** (e.g., safety/performance checkers) evaluate the system’s behavior, triggering **corrective actions** (e.g., controller adjustments) via human or automated intervention.
  The architecture emphasizes **modularity and reconfigurability**, allowing the DT to adapt to changes in the PT or its environment. Resource management is implicit, with **calibration algorithms** dynamically optimizing model parameters and **monitors** allocating computational resources to critical tasks (e.g., fault detection). The DT’s coordination mechanisms rely on **feedback loops** between the cyber (digital) and physical components, ensuring alignment through continuous data-driven updates. The constellation is **tightly coupled** for real-time applications (e.g., the incubator’s temperature control) but could extend to **loosely coupled** configurations for broader system-of-systems integration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes the following phases: **(1) Modeling Phase**: Abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created at varying levels of abstraction (e.g., CAD models for geometric properties, dynamic models for physics-based behavior via differential equations). **(2) Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework alongside real-time **data** (collected via sensors from the PT) and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, state estimation). **(3) Deployment Phase**: The DT is deployed alongside the PT, enabling real-time monitoring, simulation, and optimization. **(4) Operational Phase**: The DT continuously updates models via **calibration algorithms** to reflect the PT’s current state, using feedback loops to adjust parameters (e.g., environmental or cyber-physical system (CPS) dynamics). **(5) Evolution Phase**: The DT adapts to changes in the PT or environment, with **reconfigurability** enabling robustness. Transition management between phases involves **calibration algorithms** that reconcile model parameters with real-world data, ensuring alignment between the DT and PT. Governance is implicit through **monitoring systems** that validate safe and optimal performance, triggering human or algorithmic interventions when deviations occur (e.g., via supervisory controllers)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity** of Digital Twin (DT) models and data is governed by:
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
    - **Parameterization**: Models must include sufficient **free parameters** to capture critical dynamics (e.g., the incubator’s **four-parameter thermal model** outperformed a two-parameter version by better representing thermal inertia).
    - **Physics-Based Accuracy**: Models must align with **differential equations** (e.g., for temperature dynamics or car dynamics) to ensure predictive validity.
    - **Environmental Context**: Models must account for **uncontrolled environmental factors** (e.g., wind/road conditions for a car, ambient temperature for the incubator).
  - **Validation and Verification Methods**:
    - **Empirical Comparison**: DT predictions are validated against **real-world sensor data** (e.g., incubator temperature measurements vs. model outputs).
    - **Calibration Algorithms**: Continuously adjust model parameters using **sensor feedback** to minimize prediction errors (e.g., updating thermal coefficients in the incubator model).
  - **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**:
    - **Sensor Noise**: May introduce errors in data used for calibration.
    - **Model Simplifications**: Assumptions (e.g., linear thermal behavior) may deviate from reality.
    - **Environmental Variability**: Unmodeled factors (e.g., sudden wind gusts for a car) can reduce fidelity.
  - **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
    - **Monitors**: Actively check for deviations between DT predictions and PT performance (e.g., detecting if the incubator’s temperature drifts beyond safe thresholds).
    - **Feedback Loops**: Trigger **recalibration** or **corrective actions** (e.g., adjusting the heatbed’s power or recalibrating the car’s dynamic model).
  - **Quality Assurance Processes**:
    - **Open-Source Validation**: The incubator case study emphasizes **transparent, reproducible experiments** to verify DT techniques (e.g., comparing model predictions to hardware measurements).
    - **Iterative Refinement**: Models are refined based on **operational data** and **expert knowledge** (e.g., engineers tuning the car’s dynamic equations or the incubator’s thermal parameters)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the incubator DT system is not explicitly detailed in the documents, but key inferences can be drawn. The system involves a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)** where the **Raspberry Pi** acts as the **edge controller**, interfacing between the PT’s sensors/actuators and the DT’s computational layer. The **communication protocols** are likely **lightweight and deterministic** (e.g., **MQTT for sensor data**, **Modbus/TCP for control commands**) to ensure low latency (<100ms) and reliability. The **network topology** is probably a **star architecture** with the Raspberry Pi as the central node, connecting to sensors/actuators via **I2C, SPI, or UART** interfaces. **Bandwidth requirements** are modest (e.g., <10 kbps for temperature data) but **latency must be minimized** (<1s) to support real-time calibration and control. **Reliability mechanisms** include **redundant sensor readings** (two internal temperature sensors) and **fail-safes** (e.g., hardware watchdogs on the Raspberry Pi). **Security protocols** are not specified but may involve **encryption for command transmission** (e.g., TLS for MQTT) and **access control** to prevent unauthorized DT modifications."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for the incubator DT is implied to be **edge-centric** due to the system’s simplicity and real-time requirements. The **Raspberry Pi** serves as the **on-premise/edge hosting platform**, running the DT’s **models, calibration algorithms, and control logic** locally to minimize latency. **Computational requirements** are lightweight (e.g., solving low-order differential equations for thermal dynamics) but may scale with model complexity. **Storage requirements** are minimal, primarily for **historical sensor data** and **model parameters**. **Scalability mechanisms** are not explicitly discussed, but the modular design (e.g., separate models for cyber/physical components) suggests **plug-and-play extensibility** for additional sensors or actuators. **Deployment models** likely include **hybrid edge-cloud** (e.g., Raspberry Pi for real-time control, cloud for long-term analytics), though the documents focus on the **local DT implementation**. **Availability/reliability assurance** is achieved via **redundant hardware** (e.g., backup Raspberry Pi) and **fault-tolerant algorithms** (e.g., model fallback modes during sensor failures)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its **monitoring and control mechanisms**, as depicted in Figure 1. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation for reliable operation. **Monitors** within the DT system actively check for **safe and optimal performance** of the physical twin (PT), detecting violations that may arise from deviations in operating conditions. While the documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), the DT framework inherently relies on **real-time data integrity** from sensors (e.g., temperature sensors inside/outside the styrofoam box) and **fail-safe mechanisms** triggered by monitor-detected anomalies. The system’s **risk assessment** is implicit in its ability to predict and mitigate deviations via model-driven simulations, though no specific threat mitigation strategies (e.g., intrusion detection, secure communication protocols) are mentioned. The **safety-critical nature** of the incubator’s temperature regulation (e.g., ensuring stable conditions regardless of internal content) suggests adherence to **control-theoretic safety standards**, though no explicit compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508) are referenced."
]
